signals.idea: Add tuneable parameters to custom effects. Each effect defines a schema of user-adjustable settings (e.g. direction, spin count, color, speed) that the LLM generates alongside the effect code. These params render as controls (sliders, selects, color pickers, toggles) in both the effect creator preview and the clip properties panel. The effect component receives params at render time via an extended EffectComponentProps. Key pieces: (1) Define a ParameterSchema type (array of {name, type: 'number'|'select'|'boolean'|'color', label, default, min/max/options}). (2) LLM generates both code and parameterSchema in its response. (3) The schema is stored in the effect's resource metadata. (4) A generic ParameterControls component renders controls from any schema. (5) EffectComponentProps gets a params: Record<string,unknown> field. (6) Current param values are stored per-clip in ClipMeta (e.g. entrance.params, exit.params, continuous.params). (7) The creator preview and clip properties both render controls and pass values through.

unresolved_flags.concern: Effect metadata: the plan does not implement the user-requested generated descriptions end-to-end. It adds no `description` field to `ParameterDefinition`, does not ask the edge function for an effect-level description, and does not replace the current save path that persists `description` from the mutable prompt textbox.

Clip effect state: the plan says per-clip params live in `clip.entrance.params` / `exit.params` / `continuous.params`, but it does not cover how default values get written when a user selects or creates a parameterized effect. In the current repo, all effect-apply paths only write `{type,duration}` or `{type,intensity}`.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 2.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 2.

items: TypeScript compiles cleanly (`tsc --noEmit`) with all new types and extended interfaces
LLM-generated effects include a `parameterSchema` array in the edge function response
ParameterControls renders the correct control type (slider/select/switch/color) for each parameter definition
Creator panel preview updates in real-time when tuneable parameter values change
Parameter schema is persisted in the effect resource's JSONB metadata
Per-clip parameter values are stored in `clip.entrance.params` / `clip.exit.params` / `clip.continuous.params`
ClipPanel shows parameter controls when a custom effect with schema is selected on a clip
Effect component receives `params` prop at render time and the rendered output reflects param values
Existing effects (built-in and custom without params) continue to work unchanged
Parameter values persist across page reload (stored in timeline config JSON)
